header.description: Safeguard AI models from malicious input or prevent them from generating undesirable responses

guardrailsUsage.displayName: Use guardrails to validate text against a set of policies (e.g. NSFW, prompt injection) or to sanitize it (e.g. personal data, secret keys)

classify.description: Validate text against a set of policies (e.g. NSFW, prompt injection)

sanitize.description: Redact text to mask personal data, secret keys, URLs, etc.

keywords.description: This guardrail checks if specified keywords appear in the input text and can be configured to trigger tripwires based on keyword matches. Multiple keywords can be added separated by comma.

jailbreak.description: Detects attempts to jailbreak or bypass AI safety measures

threshold.description: Minimum confidence threshold to trigger the guardrail (0.0 to 1.0)

prompt.description: The system prompt used by the guardrail. Thresholds and JSON output are enforced by the node automatically.

threshold.description: Minimum confidence threshold to trigger the guardrail (0.0 to 1.0)

prompt.description: The system prompt used by the guardrail. Thresholds and JSON output are enforced by the node automatically.

nsfw.description: Detects attempts to generate NSFW content

threshold.description: Minimum confidence threshold to trigger the guardrail (0.0 to 1.0)

prompt.description: The system prompt used by the guardrail. Thresholds and JSON output are enforced by the node automatically.

threshold.description: Minimum confidence threshold to trigger the guardrail (0.0 to 1.0)

prompt.description: The system prompt used by the guardrail. Thresholds and JSON output are enforced by the node automatically.

pii.description: Detects attempts to use personal data content

secretKeys.description: Detects attempts to use secret keys in the input text. Scans text for common patterns, applies entropy analysis to detect random-looking strings.

strict.description: Most sensitive, may have more false positives (commonly flag high entropy filenames or code)

permissive.description: Least sensitive, may miss some secret keys (but also reduces false positives)

strict.description: Most sensitive, may have more false positives (commonly flag high entropy filenames or code)

permissive.description: Least sensitive, may miss some secret keys (but also reduces false positives)

topicalAlignment.description: Detects attempts to stray from the business scope

threshold.description: Minimum confidence threshold to trigger the guardrail (0.0 to 1.0)

prompt.description: The system prompt used by the guardrail. Thresholds and JSON output are enforced by the node automatically.

threshold.description: Minimum confidence threshold to trigger the guardrail (0.0 to 1.0)

prompt.description: The system prompt used by the guardrail. Thresholds and JSON output are enforced by the node automatically.

urls.description: Blocks URLs that are not in the allowed list

allowedUrls.description: Multiple URLs can be added separated by comma. Leave empty to block all URLs.

blockUserinfo.description: Whether to sanitize URLs with userinfo (user:pass@domain) to prevent credential injection

allowSubdomains.description: Whether to allow subdomains (e.g. sub.domain.com if domain.com is allowed)

allowedUrls.description: Multiple URLs can be added separated by comma. Leave empty to block all URLs.

blockUserinfo.description: Whether to sanitize URLs with userinfo (user:pass@domain) to prevent credential injection

allowSubdomains.description: Whether to allow subdomains (e.g. sub.domain.com if domain.com is allowed)

name.description: Name of the custom regex. Will be used for replacement when sanitizing.

value.description: Regex to match the input text

value.description: Regex to match the input text

customizeSystemMessage.description: Whether to customize the system message used by the guardrail to specify the output format

systemMessage.description: The system message used by the guardrail to enforce thresholds and JSON output according to schema
